mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to yield positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive forward returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns with a p-value above 0.10 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirms a technical recession, which forces equity market repricing and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of confirmed recession quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment signal synchronized supply-side expansion that boosts short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series models by at least 5 percent in a walk-forward test.
